---
title: "UIAO Governance Findings"
subtitle: "Operational constraints the substrate operates around"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Governance findings document constraints the UIAO substrate has identified in its environment — conditions the substrate does not control but which block or degrade capability."
aspirational: true
---

Governance findings are **operational artifacts** that document
constraints the UIAO substrate has identified in its environment.
They are not canon (they don't declare how UIAO works); they are
not pure narrative (they carry structured evidence and ownership).
They occupy a distinct artifact class established by
[ADR-030 §5.2](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/core/canon/adr/adr-030-pre-uiao-terminology-reconciliation.md).

## The contract

Every finding has five mandatory sections:

1. **Constraint** — what the substrate has identified
2. **Evidence** — primary-source citations with URLs and access dates
3. **Capability gap** — what the substrate cannot do because of the constraint, tied to specific UIAO_NNN capability statements
4. **Proposed remedy** — internal vs external action paths
5. **Ownership trail** — append-only record of who identified, escalated, and accepts responsibility

Full contract: [`docs/findings/README.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/docs/findings/README.md).

## Current findings

::: {.callout-note}
No findings have landed yet. The first concrete finding — the FedRAMP GCC-Moderate telemetry constraint (M365 Informed Network Routing unavailability) — is drafted and will land in a follow-on PR with properly-cited Microsoft Learn + agency evidence.
:::

## Status lifecycle

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Open** | Finding documented, no remediation path agreed |
| **Awaiting-External-Remediation** | Remedy requires external action (agency policy, vendor roadmap, FedRAMP boundary adjustment); substrate-side mitigation documented if available |
| **Resolved** | Remedy landed; finding describes the historical constraint and the mechanism that now handles it |
| **Withdrawn** | Finding determined to be incorrect or duplicate; reason documented |

Findings persist in this directory at all statuses — they are the
audit trail of what the substrate has operated around.

## How findings relate to other UIAO surfaces

| Surface | Decides / declares | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Canon** (`core/canon/`) | What UIAO does | UIAO_129 Application Identity Model |
| **ADR** (`core/canon/adr/`) | How UIAO decides | ADR-030 retires "V3" as canonical vocabulary |
| **Findings** (`docs/findings/`) | **What UIAO cannot do until external conditions change** | FedRAMP-INR: M365 Informed Network Routing unavailable in GCC-Moderate |
| **Narrative** (`docs/narrative/`) | Reader-facing story | Why-modernization-is-hard |
| **Series** (`docs/series/`) | Arc-level narrative | Application-Aware Networking book |

## Escalation principle

Findings operate under the principle stated by the current agency
CIO: *"Everyone owns all problems they identify."* A finding is
the substrate's way of honoring that principle — it documents a
problem the substrate has seen, names an owner, and keeps the
issue in view until it is resolved or formally withdrawn.
